content.full_text:
Fnrn Pourtcal Pnacucps CovrvussloN
P.O. Box 807 . ,128 J Street ' Sacramento, CA 95812-0807
(916) 322-5660 ' Fax (916) 322-0886
September 27,2000
Cheri Orgel
621z}th Street
Santa Monica, California 90402
Re: Your Request for Advice
Our File No. A-00-199
Dear Ms. Orgel
This letter is in response to your request for advice regarding the campaign
provisions of the Political Reform Act ("Act").1
QUESTIONS
1. Is "Yes for Kids" an acceptable name for a non-controlled committee solely
formed to promote the passage of a special tax ballot measure?
2. Is it sufficient that the only identifying information to appear on the outside
envelope of the committee's stationery and on the stationery itself is this name
and the committee's address?
3. Once the special tax measure has been assigned a letter, are you required to
change the committee's name on its mass mailings? If so, may you change
the name to "Committee for Prop _, Yes for Kids"?
4. Do you need the committee's ID number printed on any part of the stationery
besides the remit envelopes?
CONCLUSIONS
1. Before the ballot measure has been assigned an official letter, nothing in the
Act prohibits you from naming the committee, "Yes for Kids."
I See Government Code Sections 81000-91015. Commission regulations appear at Califomia
Code of Regulations, Title 2, Sections 18109-18996.


File No. A-00-199
Page No. 2
2. Before the ballot measure has been assigned an official letter, you may use the
name "Yes for Kids" as the committee name on any mass mailing that the
committee sends.
3. Within 30 days after the measure receives an official letter, you must change
the name of the committee to include the phrase "a committee for Proposition
-." 
For example, you may use the name "Yes for Kids, Committee for
Proposition 
-."
4. Nothing in the Act requires you to include your committee's ID number on a
mass mailing sent by the committee.
ANALYSIS
Committee Name
A group raising money supporting or opposing a ballot measure will qualiff as a
committee if it raises or spends $1,000 or more. (Section 82013.) A ballot measure
committee must file a statement of organization with the Secretary of State. (Section
84101.) On the statement of organization (Form 410), the committee must include its
name, and indicate whether or not it is supporting the qualification or passage of a ballot
measure. (Section 84102.)
Before a proposed ballot measure receives an official letter or a number, nothing
in the Act requires the committee supporting or opposing the measure to use a specific
name for the committee.2 However, once a ballot measure receives an official letter or
number, the committee must include in its name either "a committee for Proposition
-," 
or "a committee against Proposition 
-." 
(Section 84107.)
Accordingly, you may use the name "Yes for Kids" for your committee until the
special tax ballot measure is assigned a letter. However, once the measure receives an
identiffing leffer, you must change the name of your committee to include "a committee
for Proposition _." For example, you may change the name of the committee to:
1. "Yes for Kids, Yes on Proposition A,"
2. "Yes for Kids, Yes on A," or
3. "Yes for Kids, Yes on Measure A."
2 Unless, of course, the committee is sponsored by an industry or other identifiable group.
(Section 84102(a), see Section 82048.7.)


File No. A-00-199
Page No. 3
Please note that when you change the committee's name, you must amend the
committee's statement of organization to indicate that the committee is supporting the
passage of the measure, rather than its qualification, and to reflect the new name of the
committee.
66Mass Mailings"
No committee may send a mass mailing3 unless "the name, street address, and
city" of the committee are shown on the outside of each piece of mail. (Section 84305(a),
copy enclosed.) This information must be in no less than 6-point type and easily legible'
A post office box may be used in lieu of a street address if the committee's address is a
matter of public record with the Secretary of State. The name,.street address, and city
must also be shown on at least one insert in the mailing unless the sender of the mailing is
a single committee. (Section 843050).)
Accordingly, before the measure receives a letter, you may put "Yes for Kids" as
the name of the committee, along with the address of the committee, on the outside of
each piece of mail of a mass mailing. You are not required to include that information on
any insert in the mass mailing since the sender of the mailing is just a single committee.
In addition, nothing in the Act requires you to include the committeeis identification
number anywhere on the envelope or on any other materials enclosed in a mass mailing.
If you have any questions about this letter, please contact me at (916) 322-5660.
Sincerely,
Luisa Menchaca
Assistant General Counsel
*#Fbt,-
Staff Counsel, Legal Division
Enclosure
LM:JB:kw
3 A "mass mailing" means over 200 pieces of substantially similar pieces of mail. (Section
82041.s.)

sections.question:
1. Is "Yes for Kids" an acceptable name for a non-controlled committee solely
formed to promote the passage of a special tax ballot measure?
2. Is it sufficient that the only identifying information to appear on the outside
envelope of the committee's stationery and on the stationery itself is this name
and the committee's address?
3. Once the special tax measure has been assigned a letter, are you required to
change the committee's name on its mass mailings? If so, may you change
the name to "Committee for Prop _, Yes for Kids"?
4. Do you need the committee's ID number printed on any part of the stationery
besides the remit envelopes?

I See, Title 2, Sections 18109-18996.
2. Before the ballot measure has been assigned an official letter, you may use the

committee sends.
3. Within 30 days after the measure receives an official letter, you must change
the name of the committee to include the phrase "a committee for Proposition
-." 
For example, you may use the name "Yes for Kids, Committee for
Proposition 
-."
4. Nothing in the Act requires you to include your committee's ID number on a
mass mailing sent by the committee.

formed to promote the passage of a special tax ballot measure?

3. Once the special tax measure has been assigned a letter, are you required to
change the committee's name on its mass mailings? If so, may you change
the name to "Committee for Prop _, Yes for Kids"?

besides the remit envelopes?

CONCLUSION: 1. Before the ballot measure has been assigned an official letter, nothing in the
Act prohibits you from naming the committee, "Yes for Kids."
I See, Title 2, Sections 18109-18996.
2. Before the ballot measure has been assigned an official letter, you may use the

committee sends.
3. Within 30 days after the measure receives an official letter, you must change

-." 
For example, you may use the name "Yes for Kids, Committee for
Proposition 
-."
4. Nothing in the Act requires you to include your committee's ID number on a
